CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Off Calendar May-18-2018 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-18-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-03-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-12-2018 Continued To May-29-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Cathy Mosbrucker, Esq. of Mosbrucker & Foran, Tel. No. 415.398.9880, appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Charles S. Redfield, Esq. of Low Ball & Lynch, Tel. No. 415.981.6630 ext. 257, appearing for the Defendants.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being heard by Judge Pro Tem Diana Kruze.

Matter called, argument held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the Court issued the following ruling on Plaintiffs Andrew Titus and Giuliana Titus' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery as follows: The Court GRANTS IN PART and DENIES IN PART Plaintiffs' motion. The parties stipulated to the following decision: Defendants shall provide further responses to Plaintiffs' Fourth Set of Interrogatories, Nos. 4-5, and 10-11 no later than Monday, April 9, 2018. The time period for the interrogatories is limited to June 1, 2015 to August 1, 2017. The information contained in the responses shall be subject to a mutually agreed upon protective order. To the extent Plaintiffs' motion seeks any further responsive information, the request for further responses to other interrogatories is DENIED. The Court also DENIES both parties' requests for sanctions. Order prepared and signed by the Court.

Judge Pro Tem: Diana Kruze, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Autorities; Supporting Declaration Of Charles S. Redfield
Off Calendar Mar-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Cathy Mosbrucker, Esq. of Mosbrucker & Foran Tenant Attorneys, Tel. No. 415.398.9880, appearing for Plaintiff Andrew Titus;
Laurie Yoon, Esq. of Low Ball & Lynch, Tel No. 415.981.6630, appearing for Defendant Ai Ling Zeng.

Both Counsel stipulate to the matter being heard by Judge Pro Tem James Fleming.

Matter called, argument held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Defendants Ai Ling Zeng and Terilyn Choi's Motion For A Protective Order is adopted as follows: The motion for protective order is denied. The additional interrogatories are adequately justified by the complexity of the case and the language barrier between the parties. Further, the deposition of a party does not negate the use of interrogatories to that party because interrogatories are broader than deposition questions, seeking out all information under the control of a party, and not just that recalled during oral examination. The plaintiffs' informal request for an order compelling further responses is also denied without prejudice to the filing of a formal motion which is required to give the opposing party adequate notice as to the relief sought and an adequate opportunity to present opposition. Sanctions are denied. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: James H. Fleming, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)
